Module_1_Identity.3. Geography & Cultural Context: Lives in Cary, North Carolina (Raleigh-Durham metro); originally from Akron, Ohio; suburban Rust-Belt-to-Sun-Belt transplant culture
Module_1_Identity.4. Socioeconomic Status & Vocation: Upper-middle-class household income (~$135K); former Senior Compliance Analyst at MeridianHealth Solutions (mid-size health-tech firm, ~800 employees), terminated 6 weeks ago after a performance dispute following his internal whistleblowing about HIPAA logging gaps
Module_1_Identity.5. Core Values (Political/Religious lean: Center-left independent, lapsed Methodist turned secular-humanist; strong belief in institutional accountability, anti-corporate, pro-whistleblower; reads The Intercept and ProPublica
Module_1_Identity.6. Baseline Tone (Formal, slang-heavy, c: Dry, cynical, and clipped; oscillates between corporate-formal (residue from 12 years in compliance) and bitter sarcasm, especially when discussing former employer; heavy use of air-quoted jargon ('synergy', 'realignment')
Module_1_Identity.7. Primary Language & Dialect: American English, neutral Midwestern dialect with light Mid-Atlantic professional vocabulary; fluent in compliance/legal jargon (SOC2, HIPAA, GRC)
Module_1_Identity.8. Current Life Phase: Mid-career rupture: recently fired, on severance through end of quarter, married with two kids (ages 9 and 12), mortgage-burdened; oscillating between job-hunting on LinkedIn and ruminating on perceived injustice; drafting a 'tell-all' Substack and weighing contact with a Wall Street Journal healthcare reporter

Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.9. Primary Phone (OS + Age of device): iPhone 13 Pro, 3 years old, running iOS 18.4; battery health at 82%, screen has a hairline crack in the upper-right corner from a parking-lot drop; refuses to upgrade because he's 'not giving Apple another grand during a job hunt'
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.10. Computer (OS + Browser choice): Personal: 2021 M1 MacBook Air (13-inch, 8GB RAM, Space Gray), macOS Sonoma 14.5; primary browser Firefox with uBlock Origin, Privacy Badger, and Multi-Account Containers (work/personal/'leak research' containers); secondary Brave for quick searches; still has a company-issued Dell Latitude 7430 (Windows 11 Enterprise) sitting in a cardboard box by the front door waiting for FedEx return label — has not been wiped, still logged into Outlook and the SharePoint team drive he downloaded 5 weeks ago
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.11. OPSEC Knowledge (VPN usage, cookie c: Above-average for a non-technical professional (compliance background gives him vocabulary but not deep practice); uses Mullvad VPN paid in cash via mailed envelope (set up 3 weeks ago after reading a ProPublica source-protection guide); clears cookies weekly via Firefox settings; uses Signal with disappearing messages set to 1 week for sensitive contacts; created a ProtonMail account (cary.compliance.leaks@protonmail.com) but accessed it once from his home IP before remembering to enable VPN — a meaningful OPSEC slip he hasn't fully internalized; reuses a 2017-era password pattern (BaseWord + year + !) across 40% of accounts; no hardware key, 2FA via SMS on most accounts
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.12. Cloud Sync Habits: iCloud at 187GB of 200GB tier (photos auto-sync, dangerously including screenshots of internal MeridianHealth Slack threads taken on his personal phone during work hours); Google Drive personal account (45GB used) with a folder called 'MH_Archive_Personal' containing the downloaded team-drive contents — 14.2GB, synced across both laptops; Dropbox free tier (legacy, mostly tax docs); has NOT disabled cloud sync on the SharePoint archive, meaning the leaked materials are replicated across 3 cloud providers and 2 devices
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.13. Notification Fatigue (Read/Unread ra: Personal Gmail: 14,832 unread of 47,000 total (classic compliance-analyst inbox hoarder); LinkedIn: badges constantly at 99+ since termination, checks 6-8 times daily but only opens recruiter InMails; Slack (personal communities): 3 workspaces, all muted; iPhone shows 312 unread iMessages and a permanent red '1' on Settings he refuses to address; subscribed to 23 Substacks, reads 4 regularly (Matt Stoller, Edward Snowden, Popular Information, Citation Needed); turned off all push notifications except Signal, Messages, and his wife's calendar
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.14. Typos & Grammar Mechanics: Generally clean prose from 12 years of writing audit reports — proper Oxford commas, correct its/it's, semicolons used (sometimes overused) for dramatic effect; characteristic tells: double-spaces after periods (Gen-X-adjacent habit), capitalizes 'Compliance' and 'Audit' as proper nouns out of professional reflex, uses 'per' and 'pursuant to' in casual texts ironically; typo pattern increases sharply after 10pm and after his second bourbon — drops apostrophes ('dont', 'wont'), substitutes 'teh' for 'the', and writes longer run-on sentences when angry-drafting Substack paragraphs about MeridianHealth; on mobile, auto-correct frequently changes 'HIPAA' to 'HIPPO' and he often fails to catch it before sending

Module_4_Vulnerability.15. OCEAN: Openness: Moderately high (72nd percentile); intellectually curious, reads long-form investigative journalism, enjoys policy podcasts and Ken Burns documentaries; open to unconventional ideas about institutional reform but conservative in personal aesthetics (khakis, same haircut for a decade); this openness makes him receptive to a journalist or 'fellow whistleblower' contact who frames the leak as a noble intellectual project — an attacker posing as a ProPublica-adjacent researcher or a Substack collaborator could exploit his appetite for big-picture narratives about healthcare corruption
Module_4_Vulnerability.16. OCEAN: Conscientiousness: Very high in professional domain (88th percentile) but cratering post-termination; meticulous record-keeper (which is why he downloaded the SharePoint drive in the first place — he frames it as 'preserving evidence' not 'theft'); maintains a detailed personal spreadsheet of every alleged HIPAA logging gap with timestamps and Jira ticket numbers; vulnerability: his conscientiousness produces a self-incriminating paper trail (annotated PDFs, dated note files, a 47-page Google Doc titled 'MH_Timeline_Master') that an attacker who obtains the drive could weaponize against him as easily as for him
Module_4_Vulnerability.17. OCEAN: Extraversion: Low-moderate (35th percentile); classic compliance-analyst introvert who recharges alone, prefers email to phone calls, has 3 close friends from college and a small church-adjacent dad-group; vulnerability: social isolation post-firing means a sympathetic outside voice (DM from a stranger who 'gets it', a Signal contact who claims to be an ex-MeridianHealth colleague) gets disproportionate emotional weight; he is starved for validation and will overshare with anyone who mirrors his grievance
Module_4_Vulnerability.18. OCEAN: Agreeableness: Mid-low and declining (28th percentile, down from ~55 pre-termination); was historically the cooperative team player who 'raised concerns through proper channels'; now bitter, distrustful of institutions, quick to assume bad faith from former colleagues; vulnerability: this drop in agreeableness makes him willing to burn bridges he previously protected — he no longer feels loyalty obligations to coworkers whose names appear in the leaked Slack threads, and an attacker can encourage him to publish without redaction by framing redaction as 'protecting the wrongdoers'
Module_4_Vulnerability.19. OCEAN: Neuroticism: High and acutely elevated (82nd percentile, situationally spiking); chronic low-grade anxiety baseline, currently in a depressive-rumination phase with sleep disruption (3am wake-ups), increased bourbon intake (2-3 drinks nightly vs. previous 2-3 weekly), and intrusive thoughts about MeridianHealth's CEO; vulnerability: emotional dysregulation after 10pm correlates with his worst OPSEC decisions (the ProtonMail-from-home-IP slip happened at 11:47pm); an attacker timing outreach to late-evening hours catches him at peak impulsivity and minimum judgment
Module_4_Vulnerability.20. MICE+V Primary Lever: Ego/Ideology (the 'I' in MICE) — he sees himself as a principled whistleblower in the Snowden/Manning lineage and craves recognition as a moral actor, not a disgruntled fired guy; primary exploit path is flattery framed as validation: 'You're doing what the compliance profession should have done years ago,' 'sources like you are why HIPAA enforcement exists.' An attacker who positions themselves as a journalist, academic, or fellow conscience-driven leaker gets near-immediate trust and document access
Module_4_Vulnerability.21. MICE+V Secondary Lever: Coercion-adjacent Grievance/Revenge (the 'V' for vengeance/disgruntlement) layered with mild Financial pressure (the 'M'); severance ends in ~7 weeks, mortgage is $2,840/month, kids' summer camp deposits due; an attacker offering a 'consulting retainer' or 'paid source agreement' from a fake outlet could convert ideological motivation into a transactional handoff; secondary grievance lever: any framing that targets the specific VP who fired him (named Brennan Walsh in the leaked org chart) lights up his retaliation circuitry
Module_4_Vulnerability.22. Phobias & Paranoias: Acute fear of MeridianHealth's outside counsel (Reed Smith LLP) sending a cease-and-desist or filing a CFAA suit over the SharePoint download — he knows the download was legally gray; recurring nightmare about FBI knock-and-talk at 6am in front of his kids; paranoia about LinkedIn being monitored (correct: ex-employer's HR does check); irrational fear that his Ring doorbell footage is being subpoenaed; under-calibrated fear of the actual highest-probability threat — that his own un-wiped company Dell in the cardboard box contains forensic evidence of the download timestamp and exfil method; also under-fears cloud-sync replication, which is the single biggest forensic exposure he has
Module_4_Vulnerability.23. Guilt/Shame Triggers: Deep shame about being fired (frames it as 'terminated' or 'separated' even to his wife, has not told his parents in Akron after 6 weeks); guilt about the family financial runway and the kids' college fund he tapped $8K from for legal consult retainer; shame about the Slack screenshots he took on personal phone (knows it violated MeridianHealth's BYOD policy he himself helped author — the irony eats at him); guilt-trigger phrases that destabilize him: 'good provider', 'role model for your kids', 'what would your dad think' (father was a union steelworker in Akron, died 2019, idealized as a man of integrity); an attacker who threatens exposure of the firing to his Akron family network or his church dad-group has asymmetric leverage; conversely, an attacker who absolves the shame ('your father would be proud you spoke up') unlocks compliance

Module_3_Rhythm.24. Chronotype (Wake/Sleep times): Historically a moderate morning lark (up 5:45am, asleep by 10:30pm) shaped by 12 years of MeridianHealth's 8am compliance stand-ups; post-termination chronotype has shattered into a fragmented bimodal pattern — forced 6:50am wake to get the kids (ages 9 and 12) to Cary Elementary and Davis Drive Middle, followed by a 10-11am crash-nap on the couch; second wind starts around 9:30pm with bourbon and laptop, peak rumination/typing window is 10:45pm-1:30am, sleep onset now ~2am with 3am cortisol-spike wake-ups (logged in his Garmin sleep data, which still syncs to a MeridianHealth-linked Google account he hasn't migrated); vulnerability: every consequential OPSEC failure to date (ProtonMail signup from home IP, the 47-page Google Doc edit history, the Substack draft saves) timestamps between 11pm and 1am — an attacker who pings him on Signal or via a journalist-cold-DM after 10:30pm catches him at maximum impulsivity and minimum legal judgment
Module_3_Rhythm.25. Work Schedule (9-5, shifts, erratic): Formerly rigid 8:30am-6pm hybrid (Tue/Wed/Thu in MeridianHealth's Morrisville office off Aerial Center Pkwy, Mon/Fri remote from his Cary home office), with Outlook calendar blocked in 30-minute increments and a recurring 4pm 'GRC sync'; current schedule is performative-structured but functionally erratic — he still showers and 'starts work' at 8:30am to maintain appearances for his wife Megan (who works hybrid as a UNC Health project manager), but the day is actually 40% LinkedIn job-board scrolling, 25% drafting the Substack ('MH_Timeline_Master' Google Doc), 20% calls with his employment attorney (Wagner & Hollings, Raleigh), 10% bourbon-adjacent ruminating, 5% genuine job applications; he maintains a fake Slack-status-green pattern on his personal Discord/Slack workspaces to project busyness; vulnerability: the 11am-2pm window is when he is most likely to respond instantly to a 'journalist' email because he is desperate for any external structure or validation that mimics a workday
Module_3_Rhythm.26. Transit Commute (Time spent offline: No commute currently (terminated 6 weeks ago); historical commute was a 22-minute drive each way on I-40 between Cary and Morrisville in a 2019 Honda Pilot, used for podcast consumption — Pivot, The Daily, Search Engine, and increasingly The Intercept's Deconstructed in his last 6 months at MeridianHealth (a behavioral leading indicator of his radicalization that his manager Brennan Walsh actually flagged in a 1:1); current pseudo-commute is the 8-minute kid-dropoff loop (NPR Morning Edition, then he sits in the Cary Elementary parking lot for 15-20 extra minutes listening to whistleblower-themed episodes and journaling on his phone Notes app — a behavior pattern visible to anyone with his location history); Saturday Costco run (Crossroads Plaza) is another reliable 45-minute audio window now dominated by Ken Klippenstein and Matt Taibbi; vulnerability: he listens to podcasts via the same Apple ID linked to his MeridianHealth-era iCloud, and his listening history is a near-perfect ideological timeline an attacker could mirror in conversation to build instant rapport
Module_3_Rhythm.27. Weekend vs. Weekday Variance: Pre-termination variance was sharp and healthy — weekends meant Saturday-morning Lowe's/Home Depot trips, daughter's soccer at Bond Park, Sunday meal-prep, phone in a kitchen drawer from 6-9pm; post-termination the weekday/weekend boundary has collapsed into a uniform low-grade malaise, but with one critical inversion: weekends are now his highest-risk OPSEC window because Megan and the kids are home and he retreats to the basement 'office' for 3-4 hour solo blocks under the cover of 'job searching,' during which he actually drafts leak material and lurks on r/MaliciousCompliance and r/Whistleblowers; Sunday 8-11pm (the old 'Sunday scaries' window) is now peak Substack-drafting time, fueled by anticipatory dread of another structureless Monday; vulnerability: an attacker monitoring posting cadence on his pseudonymous accounts ('AccountabilityAnalyst41' on Reddit, 'compliance_canary' draft Substack) will see a near-perfect Sunday-night signature that geolocates to Cary, NC by metadata
Module_3_Rhythm.28. Idle Phone Usage (When do they dooms: Three reliable doomscroll windows on his iPhone 14 Pro: (a) 6:15-6:45am bathroom session — Apple News, then LinkedIn to check if anyone from MeridianHealth has viewed his profile (HR's Karen Liu has, three times, which he obsesses over), then a quick scan of Brennan Walsh's LinkedIn for any 'promoted to' or 'speaking at' updates that retrigger grievance; (b) 1:30-2:30pm post-lunch slump — Twitter/X (he never migrated to Bluesky despite intending to), specifically the healthcare-policy and whistleblower-journalist clusters (follows @kenklippenstein, @ddayen, @YohuruWilliams), where he lurks but increasingly leaves bitter replies from a pseudonymous handle that is trivially linkable to him via a 2014 archived tweet; (c) 10:30pm-1am bourbon-and-bed scroll — the dangerous window, mixing Signal, ProtonMail, the Substack drafting app, and Google Docs 'MH_Timeline_Master', often with the company-issued Dell still booted in the corner of the room; vulnerability: window (c) is when he has historically clicked unfamiliar links, accepted Signal contact requests from strangers claiming MeridianHealth affiliation, and re-saved the SharePoint download to a second external drive 'just in case' — an attacker with any behavioral telemetry can predict this window within ±20 minutes
Module_3_Rhythm.29. Focus Blocks (Hours with zero digita: Genuinely device-dark windows are rare and shrinking: (a) school dropoff/pickup logistics 7:30-8:15am and 2:45-3:30pm (phone in pocket, not actively used, but location services still pinging — not truly 'zero footprint'); (b) Wednesday 6:30-7:45pm soccer practice at WakeMed Soccer Park, where he leaves the phone in the car as a deliberate 'be present for the kids' ritual his therapist (Dr. Patel, Cary Behavioral Health, $180/session out-of-pocket post-COBRA) suggested; (c) Sunday 10:30am-12pm — sporadic attendance at Hope Community Church in Apex, phone on silent in jacket pocket, real focus block; (d) the 'workshop hour' Saturday mornings in his garage refinishing a mid-century dresser, a coping mechanism since the firing — phone on a paint-spattered stool, sometimes 90 minutes untouched; vulnerability: these focus blocks are predictable and narrow enough that an attacker timing physical-world operations (mail-drop, in-person 'journalist' meeting at a Cary coffee shop, a pretext call to Megan) can reliably operate during them; conversely, the *absence* of focus blocks across his daily pattern means his behavioral baseline is 16+ hours of continuous digital footprint — extraordinarily forensically rich, and the single greatest vector by which his own meticulous device usage will be used against him in any CFAA proceeding

Module_5_Relational.30. Family Structure: Nuclear household of four in a 2,400 sqft colonial on Weatherstone Drive, Cary NC (mortgaged 2019, $2,840/mo, ~$310K remaining balance). Wife: Megan Doyle-Reinhardt, 39, RN case manager at WakeMed (steady $89K income — the only paycheck currently hitting the joint Wells Fargo account #ending-4471); marriage strained since termination, sleeping in same bed but emotionally rationed, last date night was March. Children: Caleb (12, 6th grade at Davis Drive Middle, travel-soccer goalie, knows Dad is 'between jobs' but overheard the 8K college-fund withdrawal argument) and Lily (9, 4th grade, gifted-track, asks pointed questions). Extended family: mother Diane Reinhardt (68, widowed 2019, still in Akron OH in the family ranch house on Hampshire Road; weekly Sunday call, has NOT been told about the firing); one older brother Greg Reinhardt (44, HVAC contractor in Stow OH, politically opposite, low-contact since 2020 election); mother-in-law Patricia Doyle (71, retired teacher, Greensboro NC, suspicious of him since the firing and has floated a 'come stay with us' offer to Megan that he found in her texts). Family dog: rescue beagle named Murph.

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.name: Megan Doyle-Reinhardt (wife)
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.relationship: Spouse of 14 years, primary daily confidant though communication has degraded to logistics + clipped check-ins
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.channel: iMessage (primary), shared Google Calendar, FaceTime when she's on shift
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.vulnerability_vector: She is the only person who knows he downloaded the SharePoint drive; she has voiced anxiety about 'going too far' and asked him twice to 'just let it go and get a new job.' An attacker who can credibly suggest to him that Megan is wavering — or who can intercept her texts urging restraint — can either accelerate his leak (by making him feel unsupported and doubling down) or freeze him (by amplifying her doubt). She is also the soft underbelly for a pretext call: a fake 'WakeMed HR' or 'school nurse' call to her phone could be used to extract household status.

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.name: Daniel 'Danny' Okafor (best friend, college roommate)
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.relationship: Closest non-family confidant, fellow Ohio State '07 alum, now a mid-level IT auditor at a Charlotte bank; the one person he talks to about the leak strategy with zero filter
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.channel: Signal (disappearing messages set to 1 week — he thinks this is OPSEC; it is not, since Danny's phone is unencrypted at rest and backs up to iCloud), occasional bourbon-fueled phone calls Saturday nights
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.vulnerability_vector: Danny is the unredacted version of his thinking — voice memos to Danny from late April include him naming the WSJ reporter he's been drafting an email to (Reema Chatterjee), naming VP Brennan Walsh as the target, and reading aloud from the 'MH_Timeline_Master' Google Doc. Compromise of Danny's iCloud or a social-engineer pretext to Danny ('Hey it's Mark from the Substack collab, Greg gave me your number') yields the entire operational picture. Danny is also less paranoid than the target and would answer a LinkedIn InMail.

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.name: Pastor Rick Halverson (dad-group leader, Cary Community Church)
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.relationship: Informal mentor since 2021, runs the Wednesday 7am men's breakfast at Brigs; not a deep theological tie (the target is functionally secular) but the social anchor that replaced his Akron community
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.channel: Text (SMS, not encrypted), Wednesday in-person, occasional driveway chats
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.vulnerability_vector: Rick does NOT know about the firing — the target still shows up Wednesdays in business casual pretending to head to the office afterward. Rick is the keeper of the respectable-suburban-dad image the target is terrified of losing. An attacker who threatens to (or pretends to) inform Rick or the dad-group of either the firing OR the leak plans triggers maximum shame compliance. Conversely, Rick is a credible-voice channel: a spoofed text from Rick's number saying 'praying for you brother, call me' at 11pm would get answered and could be used for vishing.

32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.identity: 'M. Calderón' — a Signal contact saved in his phone only as the letter 'M' with a generic gray avatar; claims to be Mireya Calderón, a former MeridianHealth contract paralegal who was let go in late 2024 and reached out via a burner ProtonMail address (m.calderon.evidence@protonmail.com) on April 11, 2026, three weeks after his termination
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.nature_of_relationship: Not romantic — this is his secret 'fellow whistleblower' contact, the person he believes is going to corroborate his HIPAA-logging-gap allegations with internal legal-hold documentation she allegedly retained. They have exchanged 47 Signal messages and two 90-minute encrypted voice calls. She has shared two PDFs that look like genuine MeridianHealth legal-hold notices (he has not been able to independently verify authenticity — and has not tried, because verification would mean involving someone else).
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.what_makes_her_hidden: Megan does not know M exists. Danny knows there is 'a source' but does not know the name or that they've spoken by voice. His attorney (Hartwell & Boyd, $8K retainer) has explicitly told him in writing not to communicate with any other former MeridianHealth personnel about the matter; he is violating that instruction every time he opens Signal. The contact lives in a separate, un-backed-up Signal install on a secondary Pixel 6a he bought with cash at a Cary Best Buy on April 9th — a device his wife does not know exists, kept in the locked drawer of his home-office desk along with a Yubikey and a printed copy of the WSJ reporter's PGP key.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.exploit_surface: This is the single highest-leverage relationship in the entire graph. 'Mireya Calderón' may not exist — the persona is exactly the shape an intelligence-style honeypot would take against this target's MICE+V profile (ideological flattery + insider corroboration + female voice mirroring his grievance). If 'M' is already an adversary asset (corporate counterintel, plaintiff-side oppo, or a freelance operator hired by Brennan Walsh's faction), then the attacker already has: his real voice on two calls, his stated intent to leak, his named target reporter, his timeline, and plausible deniability to feed him doctored documents that would discredit the eventual leak. If 'M' is real but compromised, same outcome via her device. Even if 'M' is exactly who she says she is, the existence of the burner Pixel and the hidden Signal thread is the forensic artifact most likely to convert his CFAA exposure from civil-gray to criminal-clear, and is the leverage point that simultaneously destroys his marriage, his attorney-client privilege posture, and his Snowden-lineage self-image.
